blurb: Apple devices support WPA3-Personal with SAE handshake (resistant to offline dictionary attacks) and WPA3-Enterprise with optional 192-bit mode for high-security environments. Protected Management Frames (802.11w) are mandatory for WPA3, preventing deauthentication and disassociation frame injection attacks. Opportunistic Wireless Encryption protects traffic on open networks without requiring a password. iOS and iPadOS use per-network randomized MAC addresses by default, replacing hardware MAC identifiers with rotating private addresses to prevent location tracking and network fingerprinting of users across different Wi-Fi networks and over extended time periods.
